findings.title: Unbounded slice from C-supplied count in `on_wallet_block_processed`
findings.suggestedFix: Apply the same `MAX_DETAIL_COUNT` guard used in `extract_ffi_inputs`/`extract_ffi_outputs`: `if count > MAX_DETAIL_COUNT { tracing::warn!("FFI: record count {} exceeds limit", count); return; }` at the top of the `send_records` closure, before the `from_raw_parts` call.

findings.title: Missing peer list clear before adding configured peers in FFI backend
findings.suggestedFix: Before the `for peer in &peers` loop, check whether the upstream API still exposes a clear-peers function under a new name, or call the native equivalent if available. If the FFI config object guarantees an empty peer list on construction, add a comment explaining that invariant so the omission is clearly intentional.

findings.title: TransactionInstantLocked discards is_instant_send status propagation
is_trusted hardcoded false in FFI UTXO construction
WalletEvent::TransactionInstantLocked arm in map_wallet_event is completely untested
BlockProcessed with non-empty record arrays is never exercised
WalletCoreBalance::spendable() now includes unconfirmed funds
ffi_record_to_info recomputes fallback timestamp per call
ffi_balance_to_core helper has no unit test
on_wallet_block_processed send_records closure logic is untested
map_wallet_event_block_processed_emits_balance test name is misleading

rounds.judgeSummary: The `send_records` closure in FFI `on_wallet_block_processed` lacks the `MAX_DETAIL_COUNT` bound check that protects every other raw-slice creation in the file — a real unsafe gap. The peer-clear removal and missing InstantSend status propagation are the other substantive issues, though both depend on upstream behavior that may have changed. Test coverage gaps for the new event arms are real but lower severity.

findings.title: Unbounded slice from C-supplied count in `on_wallet_block_processed`
TransactionInstantLocked discards is_instant_send status propagation
`is_trusted` hardcoded `false` in FFI UTXO construction
findings.suggestedFix:
// Derive from confirmations: treat any confirmed UTXO as trusted.
is_trusted: ffi_utxo.confirmations > 0,

findings.title: Peer list not cleared before adding user peers in FFI backend
UB risk in FFI tests: `clear_borrowed_pointers` called after assertions that may panic
`on_transaction_detected`: balance ptr can be null while record ptr is non-null
on_transaction_detected silently drops events when channel send fails
ffi_record_to_info safety contract not documented at the function boundary
Inconsistent naming: ffi_record_to_info vs extract_* helper pattern
WalletEvent::TransactionInstantLocked arm in map_wallet_event is still untested
BlockProcessed with non-empty inserted/updated/matured records is never exercised
ffi_record_to_info has no direct test
on_wallet_block_processed send_records closure logic is still untested

rounds.judgeSummary: The inter-round diff only adds `clear_borrowed_pointers` for test cleanup — none of the 8 open threads from round 1 were addressed. The unbounded `from_raw_parts` in `send_records` remains unguarded despite `MAX_DETAIL_COUNT` being used just 140 lines below in the same file, and `TransactionInstantLocked` still silently drops the txid on both native and FFI paths.

findings.title: on_transaction_instant_locked discards txid — instant-send status never reaches UI
Peer list not cleared before adding configured peers in FFI backend
`is_trusted` hardcoded `false` in FFI UTXO construction
WalletCoreBalance::spendable() now silently includes unconfirmed funds
`on_transaction_detected` null-record and null-balance paths are untested

rounds.judgeSummary: Previous blocker (unbounded slice in `on_wallet_block_processed`) is fixed with a `MAX_DETAIL_COUNT` guard, and the test cleanup ordering UB is resolved. However, the native backend's `TransactionInstantLocked` arm was fixed to emit `TransactionReceived` with `is_instant_send: true`, but the FFI backend's `on_transaction_instant_locked` still only emits `BalanceUpdated` — widening the backend parity gap. Three prior warnings (peer list clear, `is_trusted` hardcoded, spendable semantics) remain unaddressed.

findings.title: ffi_balance_to_core silently discards the `total` field of FFIBalance
`keep-finalized-transactions` feature added with no migration path for existing wallet data
findings.suggestedFix: At minimum, emit a `tracing::warn!` during `load_wallet` when `wallet_transaction_history` returns `Ok([])` but the wallet already has a non-zero balance, indicating finalized records were not stored under the previous feature flag. A more robust approach is to persist a feature-flag marker in the wallet database and clear/re-index automatically when it changes.

findings.title: on_wallet_block_processed null balance branch is untested
from_record label String→Option<String> mapping is untested in native.rs

rounds.judgeSummary: Previous `is_trusted` hardcoding and missing null-path tests are addressed in the inter-round diff. The new `on_transaction_instant_locked` body emits a stub `TransactionReceived` with all-zero placeholder fields (amount=0, timestamp=0, empty addresses), which will show a ghost transaction in the UI — that's the most interesting remaining concern. The `ffi_balance_to_core` total-field findings are real but low-impact since `WalletCoreBalance::new` recomputes internally.

findings.title: on_transaction_instant_locked has no unit test
ffi_balance_to_core silently discards FFIBalance.total
ffi_balance_to_core field mapping unverified — assertions use matches! without destructuring

rounds.judgeSummary: Two of three open threads addressed with targeted tests (null-balance branch in ffi.rs, label mapping in native.rs). The `keep-finalized-transactions` migration thread remains unchanged. Main new concern: `on_transaction_instant_locked` still has zero test coverage despite sibling callbacks each having 3+ tests — this is the most significant gap given CLAUDE.md's testing requirement for backend functions.
